ui.title: Meena ki Zindagi (Meena's Life)
ui.tagline: 20 years. One life. Your choices. Can you keep her safe AND happy?
ui.householdNote: Family income together: {h}/month. Family: {name}, her husband & mother-in-law.
ui.reset: Start over? Current game will be erased.
ui.playAgain: 🔁 Play again — can her life be better?
ui.shareFail: Screenshot this card and share it on WhatsApp!
ui.installHint: Tip: In your browser menu choose 'Add to Home Screen' to install this game like an app. Works offline!
ui.savedAuto: Progress saves automatically on this phone.
ui.madeWith: Free & open source. Share it with someone who needs it. ❤️
char.meena.d: Garment factory worker in Mumbai. Came from a village in UP. Sharp, saves nothing… yet.
char.sunita.d: Beautician in a Thane parlour. Dreams big, spends bigger. Instagram expert.
char.rekha.d: Works in 4 houses in Andheri. Husband drives an auto. Strongest person you'll meet.
ch1.intro: {name} holds her first salary. ₹{p}! In the village this was 3 months of money. In Mumbai, everything feels possible… and everyone has an advice. 🎉
c1s1.q: Roommate Pinky shows her phone: "Take the new phone! Only ₹1,200 per month EMI. Reels also in HD!" {name}'s old phone works fine… mostly.
c1s1.o1: Take the EMI! Life needs a little style
c1s1.o1fb: New phone! 🤳 But now ₹1,200 leaves every month for 2 YEARS — ₹28,800 total for a ₹24,000 phone. The EMI eats first, {name} eats later.
c1s1.o2: Old phone chalega. Start a ₹500/month gullak instead
c1s1.o2fb: Boring choice? Wait and watch. ₹500/month quietly becomes a wall between {name} and every future emergency. 🪔
c1s1.o3: Buy a good secondhand phone in cash
c1s1.o3fb: Smart jugaad! ₹6,000 gone but NO monthly EMI chain. Got the phone, as well as the Freedom!
c1s2.q: Lunch break. Shanta Tai from packing dept whispers: "Beti, join our committee. ₹1,000 monthly. In lucky draw, you get ₹50,000!" Everyone calls her Chit Fund Aunty.
c1s2.o1: Join the committee! Everyone is doing it
c1s2.o1fb: Joined! Committees DO work sometimes… if the organiser is honest and stays in town. That's a big IF. No paper, no proof, no complaint possible. 🤞
c1s2.o2fb: Tai made a face 😒 but {name}'s money stays where {name} can see it.
c1s2.o3: Ask: "Tai, what about my money if you disappear?"
c1s2.o3fb: Tai laughed nervously and changed the topic. 🚩 That silence was the real answer. {name} stayed out.
c1s3.q: A camp outside the factory: "Make e-Shram card — FREE! Government ID for workers. Accident insurance included." The line is long and it's hot. 🥵
c1s3.o1fb: 30 minutes of sweat = a government ID that unlocks schemes, accident cover, and proof she EXISTS in the system. Best free thing in Mumbai. 🪪✨
c1s3.o2: "I will do it tomorrow" (spoiler: Tomorrow never comes)
c1s3.o2fb: The camp left the next day. 2 years pass in a blink. 😅
c1s3.o3: Skip it, go for vada pav with friends.
c1s3.o3fb: The vada pav was legendary 🤤. The card would have been more legendary. Priorities, {name}!
c1s4.q: Phone rings. Bhaiya from the village: "Behen, festival is coming, WE need to save our face. Please send INR 5000."
c1s4.o1fb: Family happy 😊, gullak empty. Family matters — but if she says yes to everything, who says yes to HER future?
c1s4.o2: Send ₹2,500: "Bhaiya, that is all I can do"
c1s4.o2fb: Bhaiya grumbled for 2 minutes, then said FINE. Boundaries is also a form of love. 💪
c1s4.o3fb: The call ended coldly. 😔 Saying no has a cost too — tension. There's no perfect answer here, only trade-offs.
ch2.intro: One year in Mumbai done! {name} has some money now and a big question: Where to save the money? Everyone has an opinion- even the milkman!
c2s1.q: {name}'s savings are growing. Pinky says "Keep it under your mattress. The bank is a thief!" The post office uncle says something about 'RD'. What to do?
c2s1.o1: Under the mattress. Your money with yourself.
c2s1.o1fb: Safe from banks, not safe from rats, rain, theft… or {name}'s own shopping moods. And it grows by exactly ₹0. 🐀
c2s1.o2fb: Good — safe and earns ~4%. But money lying in savings gets spent. Money that leaves automatically gets SAVED.
c2s1.o3: Post office RD — ₹1,000 auto-cut every month
c2s1.o3fb: Government-backed, 6.7% interest, and the best part: the money leaves BEFORE she can spend it. Discipline on autopilot. 📮🔥
c2s2.q:
Bank mein ek mela: 2 government schemes.
"₹436 per year = ₹2 LAKH ka life insurance (PMJJBY). 
₹20 per year = ₹2 lakh accident cover (PMSBY)." {name}: "So cheap? Must be a scam."
c2s2.o1fb: ₹456 a YEAR — that's less than one pizza — for ₹2 lakh protection. It's real, it's government-backed, it auto-cuts from the account. Biggest no-brainer in this game. 🛡️
c2s2.o2: Only the ₹20 one. Other one sounds risky
c2s2.o2fb: ₹20 for accident cover taken — good start! But the ₹436 life cover protects the family from the BIGGEST shock. Half shield is half protection.
c2s2.o3: "Insurance is money wasted. I am invincible"
c2s2.o3fb: Famous last words in every serial. 😬 Insurance feels like waste — until the one day it's the only thing standing between family and ruin.
c2s3.q: Neighbour aunty got FREE heart surgery with an 'Ayushman card'. Free?! ₹5 lakh per year per family, government hospital scheme (PM-JAY). Checking eligibility takes one visit.
c2s3.o1: Go check eligibility & make the card
c2s3.o1fb: Card is made! ₹5 lakh/year of hospital cover for the whole family. Crores of eligible families never check. {name} checked. 🏥👑
c2s3.o2: "I will never need to go to the Hospital"
c2s3.o2fb: Warding off the evil eye 🧿 …but hospitals don't send invitations. They send bills.
c2s4.q: GOOD NEWS! Supervisor: "{name}, your work is good. ₹2,000 raise!" Pinky already has plans for it. So does {name}'s landlord, probably.
c2s4.o1: Put the raise into RD before it disappears
c2s4.o1fb: The golden rule: Increase your savings in line with your earnings. {name} won't even miss money she never saw. 🧠
c2s4.o2: Upgrade life a little — she's earned it!
c2s4.o2fb: New kurtis, better tiffin, some fun. Living a little matters too — as long as the OLD savings keep running. Balance.
c2s4.o3fb: A mother's blessing is the biggest return 🙏 — and family goodwill is real security too. Just don't let YOUR pot stay empty.
ch3.intro: Mumbai monsoon. Romantic in movies. In real life? The sky opens… and life throws its first REAL test at {name}. ⛈️
ev.ev_dengue.b: Maa comes to visit, and the mosquito strikes. High fever, platelets falling, private hospital: "Admit karna padega. Around ₹35,000."
ev.ev_flood.b: The worst rain in years. {name}'s kholi floods — mattress, stove, clothes, phone charger, sab gaya. Rebuilding needs about ₹25,000.
ev.free: 🛡️ AYUSHMAN CARD ZINDABAD! The hospital bill: ₹0. That boring card just saved the family. {name} walks out with Maa and her savings intact.
ev.payout: 🛡️ Insurance payout received: ₹{amt}! The shield worked exactly when it was needed.
ev.paidCash: The gullak took the hit: ₹{amt} gone. It hurts… but THIS is why the emergency fund existed. No beggar's bowl, no moneylender. 💪
ev.shortfall: Savings weren't enough. ₹{amt} is still needed — and it's needed TODAY. Whom to ask?
fund.q: ₹{amt} needed. Right now. These are the options:
fund.sahukarfb: Instant money, invisible chains. 3% per month means the debt GROWS faster than {name} can run. This hole gets deeper while she sleeps. 🕳️
fund.appfb: 2-minute money, 24x7 harassment. App loans charge like sahukars but shame you on WhatsApp too. The debt is real either way.
fund.chit: Ask Chit Fund Tai for the committee money early
fund.chitfb: Tai gave the money — minus a 20% 'adjustment'. 🙄 At least it's not debt. That's how committees work: your money, her rules.
fund.goldfb: The gold went for less than it was bought (making charges are a one-way toll). But it saved the day — that's what assets are for.
fund.sipfb: SIP redeemed in 2 days. This is why SIP > gold locker > committee: when trouble comes, the money actually COMES.
c3s2.q: The storm passed. {name} stares at the ceiling that night, thinking: "Next time…?" What does she promise herself?
c3s2.o1: Build an emergency fund — ₹500/month minimum, no matter what
c3s2.o1fb: Target: 3 months of expenses, kept boring and reachable. An emergency fund isn't an investment — it's a seatbelt. 🪔
c3s2.o2: Get that ₹456 insurance FIRST thing tomorrow
c3s2.o2fb: Done — ₹2 lakh life + accident cover for the family. Never again 'I wish I had bought it'.
c3s2.o3: "We will figure out when it happens"
c3s2.o3fb: {name}, dear… 'We will figure out' is not a plan, it's a prayer. The game continues. So does the risk. 😬
ch4.intro: Two weddings this year — cousin's AND devar's. In India, weddings are where love, respect and budget always at odds. 🥊💒
c4s1.q: The jeweller near the station: "Madam, GOLD SCHEME! 11 installments do, 12th FREE!" Everyone in the building is doing it. Cousin's wedding gift is due too.
c4s1.o1fb: The '12th free' sounds like 9% return — but making charges (8-25%!) quietly eat it. And you can ONLY buy jewellery from HIM. It IS gold, but it isn't cheap. ✨
c4s1.o2: Buy a small gold coin now, keep it simple
c4s1.o2fb: A coin (not jewellery) skips heavy making charges. Gold protects against bad times, grows slowly. Old-school, not stupid.
c4s1.o3: Gift ₹2,001 cash with love, skip the gold race
c4s1.o3fb: Shagun done, respect almost intact, budget alive. The wedding was lovely. Nobody checked the gift's weight. 🧧
c4s2.q: Devar's wedding. In-laws hint LOUDLY: "A big household should give big gifts…" What will people say pressure = 100.
c4s2.o1: Take a ₹30,000 loan. It is about our respect!
c4s2.o1fb: The function was grand for 1 day. The loan will attend the family for YEARS, growing every month. People forget after 2 days, the debt doesn’t forget. 💸
c4s2.o2: Give a modest ₹5,000 gift, absorb the taunts
c4s2.o2fb: Two aunties taunted. It stung. But 'what will people say' doesn't pay EMIs — {name} does. Respect built on debt is rent, not ownership. 🛡️
c4s2.o3: Sit with husband, agree on ₹10,000 together
c4s2.o3fb: They decided TOGETHER — and presented a united front. A couple that plans money together, survives weddings together. 🤝
c4s3.q: Husband's eyes are shining: "New bike? Only ₹60,000. The loan is easy to get. Better than the auto!"
c4s3.o1fb: Vroom vroom 🏍️ …every month ₹2,800 EMI bhi vroom vroom. A bike loses value the day it's bought; the loan doesn't.
c4s3.o2: Buy a good secondhand bike in cash.
c4s3.o2fb: ₹25,000, no EMI, same wind in the hair. Secondhand earns no less respect on the highway.
c4s3.o3fb: He sulked for a week 😤. But saving-then-buying vs loan-then-crying is THE habit that separates the two endings of this game.
ch5.intro: A baby girl! 👶🌸 The kholi is louder, sweeter, and more expensive. Suddenly the future 20 years away feels very, very real.
c5s1.q: At the post office for the RD, the clerk says: "You had a daughter? Take the Sukanya Samriddhi — 8.2% interest, the highest there is, for your daughter." That evening an LIC agent uncle also visits with a 'special child plan'.
c5s1.o1fb: 8.2% — the HIGHEST government-guaranteed rate anywhere, tax-free, locked for her daughter's education/wedding. The single best scheme in this game. 🌸👑
c5s1.o2: "She is still young, we will see"
c5s1.o2fb: SSY can only be opened before she turns 10 — and every year of 8.2% compounding lost never comes back. 'Later' is expensive.
c5s1.o3: Agent uncle's child plan — "Your money will be back, insurance additional!"
c5s1.o3fb: Plans that mix insurance + investment usually do BOTH badly (~4-5% return, heavy lock-in, agent gets fat commission). That's why uncle came to YOUR house. 🧐
c5s2.q: Maternity break — 6 months of lower income. The RD auto-cut is due and money is tight. Post office uncle offers options.
c5s2.o1: Pause new savings for a few months, protect the base
c5s2.o1fb: Pausing is PLANNING, not failing. The old money keeps growing untouched. Restart when income returns. 🧘‍♀️
c5s2.o2: Break the RD, take the money out
c5s2.o2fb: Money came, minus a premature-closure haircut. Sometimes needed — but breaking savings for regular months (not emergencies) becomes a habit fast.
c5s2.o3: Borrow ₹15,000 'chhota sa' from sahukar to save face
c5s2.o3fb: Borrowing at 36%/year to SAVE at 6.7%?! That's filling the gullak with one hand and emptying it with a shovel. 🕳️
c5s3.q: Saas declares: "Please save gold for your daughter. These governemnt papers are nothing." She means well. She also stores cash in a steel dabba.
c5s3.o1fb: Gold is family tradition and real backup. Just remember: it earns nothing monthly, and selling it in crisis always fetches less than the sticker.
c5s3.o2: "Maa, SSY gives 8.2% interest." — put it there.
c5s3.o2fb: Saas was suspicious until {name} showed the passbook printing interest every year. Sarkar-backed, beti ke naam. Even the dabba approved. 🌸
c5s3.o3: Half gold (for Saas), half SSY (for math)
c5s3.o3fb: Ghar ki shanti + beti ka future. That's not luck, that's smart peace-making. 🕍
ch6.intro: Year 10. Income is better, the daughter is in school, and suddenly EVERYONE has a 'scheme' for {name}'s money. Beware the shiny things. ✨🪤
c6s1.q: Jethji is GLOWING: "₹50,000 lagao, 2 saal mein DOUBLE. Guaranteed! Company is form foreign. Even I have put in!"
c6s1.o1fb: First payout came ON TIME 🤩 — that's the hook. Real 'double guaranteed' doesn't exist; schemes pay early birds with new birds' money. Watch this space…
c6s1.o2fb: 'Only' ₹10,000 — that's 20 RD months! The first payout will come, they ALWAYS pay first. That's how they reel in the ₹50,000 later. 🪝
c6s1.o3: "Whatever doubles your money doubles the cheating too" — refuse.
c6s1.o3fb: Jethji called her a coward. Rule of the game: guaranteed high return = guaranteed lie. Government deposits pay 7-8%; someone promising 100% — WHY? 🚫🧠
c6s2.q: Colleague Rupa shows her phone: "₹500 monthly SIP, in a mutual fund. For 10 years. Look how much it became." The number is… surprisingly big.
c6s2.o1fb: SIP = auto-invest in the market monthly. Some years down, most years up — long term averages ~10-12%. ₹500/month for 10 yrs ≈ ₹1.1-1.2 lakh from ₹60,000 put in. 🌱
c6s2.o2fb: Buying ONE stock on a tip is gambling. A SIP in a diversified fund for 10+ years is how crores of ordinary Indians now grow money. Not knowing the difference costs lakhs.
c6s2.o3: Ask Rupa to help set up ₹1,000/month
c6s2.o3fb: Set up in 15 minutes on a SEBI-regulated app. Rupa's rule: 'Start the SIP, delete the app, open it after 10 years.' 🌳
c6s3.q: Every cricket match, a WhatsApp forward: "Cricket betting group — daily winners!" (Real-money fantasy apps got banned by law in 2025 — but illegal betting rings just moved to Telegram and WhatsApp instead.) Husband's friend 'won' ₹3,000 once. He doesn't mention the ₹40,000 he's put in.
c6s3.o1: Join the betting group — it's only ₹100
c6s3.o1fb: ₹100 daily = ₹36,000/year, quietly. These groups aren't just risky, they're illegal — no complaint, no recovery, no protection if the operator vanishes with everyone's money. The house always wins. 🎰
c6s3.o2: Ignore the messages, leave the group
c6s3.o2fb: Correct. Something illegal AND designed to take your money is not a shortcut — it's a trap with two locks on it. 📵
c6s3.o3: Also warn chacha who's been playing daily
c6s3.o3fb: Chacha got defensive, then quietly checked his transaction history, then went pale. You saved a family some real pain. 🗣️❤️
c6s4.q: Bank manager: "{name} ji, Atal Pension Yojana — pay ₹210/month now, after 60 get ₹5,000 pension EVERY month, guaranteed, for life." Old age suddenly enters the chat.
c6s4.o1fb: For unorganised workers there's no company pension — APY IS the pension. Government-guaranteed. Future-{name} will touch present-{name}'s feet. 🌅
c6s4.o2fb: That's exactly why it's cheap NOW. Join late and the monthly amount jumps up. Old age comes for everyone — pension only for planners.
c6s4.o3fb: Smart start! Even a small guaranteed pension means no begging from anyone in old age. Can upgrade later. 🌄
ch7.intro: 15 years in Mumbai. The daughter is in school, life has a rhythm. And then — the phone rings. Some years test everything you've built. ☎️⛈️
ev.ev_accident.b: Husband's auto hit by a tempo. He's alive 🙏 but the leg needs surgery + 3 months of rest. Hospital estimate: ₹90,000. His income: zero for 3 months.
ev.ev_joblose.b: The factory shifts to another state overnight. "Sab ko nikala." 4 months pass before {name} finds equal work. No salary, full expenses.
ev.ev_death.b: A call no one should get. {name}'s husband — an accident on the highway. He's gone. 🕯️ Beyond the grief, the house now runs on one income, and last rites cost ₹30,000.
ev.death.pmjjby: Amid the darkness, the bank calls: that ₹436-a-year family life cover (PMJJBY) pays ₹2,00,000. It doesn't fill the chair at dinner. But the daughter stays in school, the room stays theirs, and grief doesn't have to become debt. 🕯️🛡️
ev.death.nopolicy: There is no policy. There is no payout. Grief comes with a begging bowl — relatives, sahukar, whoever will give. This is the ₹436 that was never paid. 🕯️
c7s2.q: In the middle of the crisis, the school fee notice arrives: ₹15,000 for the daughter's year. The neighbour says "She's a girl — keep her home, save the money."
c7s2.o1: Pay the fees. Education is non-negotiable
c7s2.o1fb: The single highest-return investment in this entire game is that girl finishing school. Every rupee here multiplies for 50 years. 📚👑
c7s2.o2: Pull her out this year, "We will see later."
c7s2.o2fb: One 'temporary' break — and statistically most girls never return. The saving today is tiny; the cost is her whole earning life. This wound shows up in the ending. 💔
c7s2.o3: Hunt for scholarships with the e-Shram/labour card
c7s2.o3fb: Because {name} was IN the system, the system had doors: labour-card education aid cut the cost. Registered workers' kids have schemes most people never claim. 🔍✨
ch8.intro: 20 years since that first salary. Grey strands in the hair, a teenage daughter with opinions 🙄, and decisions that now shape RETIREMENT, not just next month.
c8s1.q: Bhaiya calls about the village land: "A builder has a proposal? Or do we give it to the cousin for agriculture?"
c8s1.o1fb: Cash in hand feels powerful — and disappears the fastest of all assets. Land sold in a hurry is sold cheap, and it never comes back.
c8s1.o2: Lease to cousin for ₹2,000/month rent with written agreement.
c8s1.o2fb: The land now SENDS money instead of just sitting there. An asset that pays monthly is a pension with soil. 🌱
c8s1.o3fb: No harm done — but idle land earns nothing and invites 'kabza' headaches. Assets should work, not nap.
c8s2.q: The daughter is 13 and topping her class 🏆. Her SSY/college fund is the question of the decade now.
c8s2.o1: Boost the SSY with a ₹10,000 lump sum
c8s2.o1fb: Every rupee at 8.2% now = college fees, not college LOANS, at 18. The girl who was born in Chapter 5 is going places. 🚀
c8s2.o2: It's on track, let it be
c8s2.o2fb: It is steady. The compounding is doing its silent work.
c8s2.o3: No SSY was opened — start a dedicated RD for her now
c8s2.o3fb: SSY's door closed at age 10 — that ship sailed. But the RD starts TODAY. The best time was 8 years ago; the second-best time is now. 📮
c8s3.q: Sunday. The new girl in the chawl — fresh from the village, first salary in hand, eyeing a phone EMI — asks {name}: "Didi, how do you manage your money?"
c8s3.o1: Sit her down with chai, tell her everything
c8s3.o1fb: The circle completes. 🫖 One woman's knowledge, shared, protects a whole community. (Psst — you can do this too. Share this game.)
c8s3.o2fb: Fair enough — not everyone's a teacher. But somewhere a phone EMI just got signed… 😅
album.good: The kholi has a fridge now, and {name}'s sleep is deep. Photos on the wall: smiling, unposed.
album.mid: Managing. The room is the same, the smiles are real but tired. One bad month could shake things.
album.bad: The room has been emptied — things sold, things pawned. {name}'s smile in photos doesn't reach her eyes. The moneylender's chit sits under the mattress.
news.chitCollapse.b: Shanta Tai's committee collapsed — Tai left for the village 'permanently'. {name}'s committee money: GONE. No receipt, no case, no recovery. This happens to lakhs of people every year.
news.chitPaid.b: {name}'s turn came — the committee paid the full pot! Honest committees do exist… she got lucky with Tai. (Not everyone in this game does. Replay and see.)
news.scamGone.b: The 'money double' office is a locked shutter. Phones off. Jethji is not making eye contact. The invested money is gone forever. The early 'profit' was the bait — classic Ponzi.
news.scamDodged.b: News spreads: the 'money double' company vanished with the whole neighbourhood's money. Jethji lost ₹50,000. {name}'s refusal now looks wise. It wasn't luck — it was one rule: guaranteed double = guaranteed scam.
end.tier1.b: The debt got in early and never left — it grew every month while sleep shrank. At 45, {name}'s BP is high, the room is bare, and the sahukar's number is still the most-dialled. This life deserved better choices. It can have them — replay.
end.tier2.b: No debt — that's real. But no cushion either. Every month starts at zero; one hospital bill or one bad monsoon stands between this life and the fall. Surviving is not the same as safe.
end.tier3.b: Insured, funded, steady. When shocks came, they hit a wall of savings and policies instead of hitting the family. {name} sleeps well. The daughter dreams big. This is what boring choices build. 🛡️
end.tier4.b: Emergency fund ✔ Insurance ✔ SIP & schemes compounding ✔ Pension locked ✔. At 45, {name} is the woman OTHERS come to for advice — with chai and a passbook. From ₹0 saved to a fortress. 👑
end.ail.low: Health: strong. Some grey hair, all of it earned gracefully. 💪
end.ail.mid: Health: the years of tension left BP medicines on the shelf. Manageable, but the body kept the score.
end.ail.high: Health: chronic BP, diabetes, aching joints — decades of money-tension written on the body. Stress compounds too.
end.edu: 🎓 The daughter is in college — the first in the family. Her salary will one day dwarf every number in this game.
end.eduPulled: 💔 The daughter never returned to school after that year. Her lost earnings are this story's biggest invisible number.
end.mentor: 🫖 Three women in the chawl now have RDs and ₹456 insurance because of {name}'s Sunday chai lessons.
end.widow: 🕯️ She carried the family alone after that terrible year — and the family held.
lesson.efund: 🪔 An emergency fund is a seatbelt — build it BEFORE the accident.
lesson.ins456: 🛡️ ₹456/year buys life + accident cover of ₹2 lakh each (the bank schemes PMJJBY + PMSBY). Cheapest protection in India.
lesson.pmjay: 🏥 Check Ayushman (PM-JAY) eligibility — ₹5 lakh/year hospital cover, FREE.
lesson.ssy: 🌸 Daughter under 10? Sukanya Samriddhi @8.2% is unbeatable. Open before it's too late.
lesson.sip: 🌱 SIP small, SIP long, don't peek. ₹500/month grows into lakhs over 15-20 years.
lesson.scam: 🚫 'Guaranteed double' = guaranteed dhokha. Govt gives 7-8%. Anything wildly above: RUN.
lesson.debt: 🕳️ Sahukar/app loans at 30%+ dig faster than you can climb. Avoid, or repay FIRST.
lesson.apy: 🌅 No company pension? Atal Pension Yojana IS your pension. ₹210/month → ₹5,000/month at 60.
share.text: I guided {name} through 20 years of life in '{name} ki Zindagi' — ending: {tier}! Savings: {a}. Can you do better? Play free:
share.cardBottom: Can you do better? Play free!
lm.efund.b: 3 months of expenses, kept aside in a bank account and NEVER touched except for true emergencies. If monthly expenses are ₹15,000 → target ₹45,000. Start with ₹500/month. It's not an investment — it's the wall between a bad day and a debt trap.
lm.chit.t: Committee / Chit Fund — the real story
lm.chit.b: Informal committees run on pure trust: no receipt, no regulator, no recovery if the organiser vanishes (and every year, many do). Registered chit funds exist, but the mohalla-wali committee is a handshake, not a guarantee. Post office RD gives 6.7% with a government stamp.
lm.eshram.b: Free government registration for unorganised workers (maid, driver, factory, delivery, tailor — sab). Gets you an ID, accident insurance cover, and entry into welfare schemes & scholarships. One-time, takes minutes at a CSC centre or online.
lm.rd.b: Fixed amount auto-saved monthly for 5 years at ~6.7%, government-backed. ₹1,000/month ≈ ₹71,000+ at maturity from ₹60,000 deposited. The magic isn't the rate — it's that the money leaves before you can spend it.
lm.jansuraksha.b: PMJJBY: ₹436/year → ₹2 lakh to family if you die (any reason). PMSBY: ₹20/year → ₹2 lakh on accidental death/disability. Auto-debits from any bank account, ages 18-50/70. Enroll via bank app, branch, or post office. Less than ₹1.25/day for both.
lm.pmjay.b: ₹5 lakh PER FAMILY PER YEAR of free hospital treatment at empanelled hospitals. Crores of low-income families are eligible and don't know it. Check eligibility with your ration card/Aadhaar at a hospital Ayushman desk or CSC centre. If eligible — this is your biggest shield.
lm.gold.t: Jeweller Gold Schemes — the fine print
lm.gold.b: '11 do, 12th free' ≈ 8-9% return, BUT you must buy jewellery from that shop, where making charges (8-25%) eat the bonus. Coins/bars carry lower charges than jewellery. Gold is decent backup — just know: it pays no monthly income and sells below sticker price in a hurry.
lm.ssy.b: For a daughter below 10: 8.2% — the highest government-guaranteed rate in India, tax-free. Min ₹250/year. Deposit 15 years, matures when she's 21 (partial withdrawal for education at 18). Open at any post office or bank. If you have a young daughter, this beats everything.
lm.scam.b: Every Ponzi works the same: pay early investors with new investors' money, build trust, collect big, vanish. Warning signs: guaranteed high returns, pressure to bring friends, unregistered company, payouts in cash. Rule: sarkari schemes give 7-8%. Anyone promising 50-100% is stealing, slowly.
lm.sip.b: Auto-invest a fixed amount monthly into a mutual fund. Markets swing year to year, but 10-20 year averages have been ~10-12% for diversified funds. ₹500/month × 20 years ≈ ₹5 lakh from ₹1.2 lakh invested. Use only SEBI-registered apps/funds. Long game only — don't peek, don't panic.
lm.apy.b: Government-guaranteed pension for unorganised-sector workers (18-40 to join). Pay a small monthly amount till 60 → fixed pension ₹1,000-₹5,000/month for LIFE, spouse continues after you. At ~30, ₹5,000-pension costs about ₹577/month; younger = cheaper. Enroll at any bank/post office.
ui.collectionHint: Every run plays out differently. Can you unlock all four endings?
c8s2p.q: The daughter is 13. Six years since that school year she never went back to — she helps at home now, and sometimes joins the tailor next door to learn stitching. One evening a neighbour's girl mentions: "NIOS — Anyone, at any age, can pass 10th standard. My aunty gave it at 32 years old."
c8s2p.o1fb: NIOS (National Institute of Open Schooling) is real, government-run, and has no age limit — lakhs write their 10th/12th this way every year. Six years lost is real. But this door was never actually locked. 📖🌟
c8s2p.o2: Enrol her in tailoring training instead — ₹2,000
c8s2p.o2fb: A trade in her hands is real security too — many successful boutique owners started exactly here. Not every good future needs a certificate on the wall. 🧵
c8s2p.o3: Things are fine as they are, leave it
c8s2p.o3fb: No judgment — this is real life, not a test. But {name} lies awake some nights wondering what her daughter's 13 could have looked like. That thought is worth carrying into whatever comes next. 🤷‍♀️
intro.what: A girl comes to Mumbai to work. For 20 years, YOU will take her money decisions — save, spend, insure, invest. Her life will change with every choice you make.
intro.how1: 👆 Read the situation, tap the choice you feel is right. No right answers forced on you!
intro.how2: 🪔 The gullak (piggy bank) on top fills as savings grow. ❤️ health, 😰 tension, 🛡️ safety — watch these three too.
intro.how3: 💯 No real money here — it's a game! Your progress saves by itself. Play again and again, try different choices, see different lives.
scheme.pmjjby: PMJJBY = life insurance of ₹2 lakh for just ₹436/year — if something happens to the earner, family gets ₹2,00,000.
scheme.pmsby: PMSBY = accident insurance of ₹2 lakh for just ₹20/year — less than one chai per month.
scheme.pmjay: PM-JAY (Ayushman Bharat) = free hospital treatment up to ₹5 lakh per year for the whole family — the card is free.
scheme.apy: APY (Atal Pension Yojana) = save a little every month now, get a fixed pension of up to ₹5,000/month after age 60.
scheme.ssy: SSY (Sukanya Samriddhi) = a savings account for your daughter with high government-guaranteed interest, for her studies and future.
scheme.eshram: eShram = free government card for workers — brings accident cover and makes it easier to get other scheme benefits.
fund.cashfb: This is EXACTLY why savings exist. No interest, no folded hands before anyone. The gullak got lighter — but the family stayed out of debt. Fill it back slowly.
fund.surplus: We have more than 6 months' expenses saved — use the extra 💪
fund.surplusfb: Smart! You kept 6 months of expenses untouched as the safety floor and used only the surplus. This is how strong families handle shocks — no 30% interest, no sleepless nights.
fund.partial: Give whatever savings we have, borrow only the rest
fund.partialfb: Good thinking — every rupee of your own is a rupee you don't pay interest on. The borrowed part is now smaller and easier to repay.
mile.fan: 1 month's expenses saved — a new fan! Hot Mumbai nights just got easier. 🌀
mile.bed: 3 months' expenses saved — a proper bed has come home! No more sleeping on the floor. 🛏️
mile.tv: 6 months' expenses saved — a TV! The whole family watches serials together now. This 6-month fund is your real safety net. 📺
mile.fridge: 9 months' expenses saved — a fridge! Cold water in summer, vegetables stay fresh. ❄️
mile.wash: 12 months' expenses saved — a washing machine! {name}'s hands finally get some rest. 🧺
mile.paint: 18 months' expenses saved — fresh paint on the walls! Neighbours ask: 'What's your secret?' Answer: little by little, every month. 🎨
intro.welcome: Welcome to the story of Meena.
ui.disclaimer: Note: All characters and situations in this app are fictitious. Any resemblance to any real person is purely co-incidental.
